suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note about the Droid-Shield pre-commit hook flagging test fixtures with sensitive-looking field names (AccessToken, RefreshToken, etc.). Workers should use individual field assignments with comments like '// test fixture' to avoid false positives.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: oauth-token-store worker had to restructure test fixture code to work around Droid-Shield false positives on AccessToken/RefreshToken field assignments.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Relax the 'Write Tests First (Red)' TDD requirement for cases where tests and implementation are tightly coupled (e.g., new files with mock server infrastructure). Multiple workers deviated here because tests required mock servers that depended on implementation structure.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three workers (oauth-config-and-registry, copilot-backend, github-token-discovery) deviated from the TDD step, writing tests alongside or after implementation due to tight coupling with mock server infrastructure.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker used heredoc (cat > file) instead of Create/Edit tools for writing files
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a tool usage preference, not a codebase convention. The AGENTS.md does not specify tool preferences and this doesn't affect code quality.
Already documented in the codebase and not a new discovery requiring library documentation.

rejectedObservations.observation: uuid package available as indirect dep
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in go.mod and not actionable guidance — workers should run 'go mod tidy' which handles this automatically.

rejectedObservations.observation: Copilot/codex use OpenAI backend as placeholder
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in .factory/library/oauth-config-and-registry.md by the implementing worker.
Already documented in .factory/library/github-token-discovery.md under 'Design Decisions'. Not a new finding.
